stemHtml: CS-23 and CS-25 use the term "take-off flight path" to describe certain performance elements. Considering gross aircraft performance; the "take-off flight path" (gradient) is the...

options.a: actual flight path of the aeroplane with safety margins included.
options.b: actual flight path of the aeroplane with NO safety margins applied.
options.c: correction applied to flight path gradient to correct for non-ISA conditions.
options.d: minimum flight path to clear the most restrictive obstacle(s) by exactly 35 ft.
